preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [eo] to the close-mid front unrounded vowel [e] is a vowel reduction process documented in Germanic languages. This process eliminates the glide element while preserving the primary vowel quality and represents a common pathway in diphthong simplification.

examples.note: Monophthongization of Old English diphthongs in unstressed syllables and word-medial position.
Continuation of diphthong reduction process from Old English inheritance.
Diphthong reduction in certain German dialects, particularly in rapid speech.
Historical monophthongization in Old Swedish to Middle Swedish transition.
